steps.title: Calculate the mean
steps.content:
From the frequency table, the total frequency is $a + 2a + 3a + 2a + a = 9a$.

The sum of all values weighted by frequency is:
$$1(a) + 2(2a) + 3(3a) + 4(2a) + 5(a) = a + 4a + 9a + 8a + 5a = 27a$$
Therefore, mean $= \frac{27a}{9a} = 3$.

steps.title: Find the median
steps.content:
With $9a$ data points (an odd count), the median is at position $\frac{9a+1}{2}$.

Find cumulative frequencies:
  • Through value 1: $a$ data points
  • Through value 2: $a + 2a = 3a$ data points
  • Through value 3: $3a + 3a = 6a$ data points
For positive $a$, we have $3a \lt \frac{9a+1}{2} \lt 6a$ (since $9a + 1 > 6a$ and $9a + 1 \lt 12a$). The median position falls in the value 3 group, so the median is 3.

steps.title: Find the difference
steps.content: Mean $-$ Median $= 3 - 3 = 0$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
